note_data.title: Cell structure and organisation
keyPoints: Living organisms undergo seven key processes: Movement, Respiration, Sensitivity, Growth, Reproduction, Excretion, and Nutrition
Organisms are classified into five kingdoms: Animal, Plant, Fungus, Prokaryote and Protoctist
All cells contain cytoplasm, cell membrane, DNA, ribosomes and enzymes as basic features
Vertebrates contain a backbone while arthropods have an exoskeleton and segmented body
Plant kingdom is split into flowering and non-flowering plants, with flowering plants divided into dicotyledons and monocotyledons
Viruses are not classified as living as they cannot complete life processes and are non-cellular

definitions.definition: A group of organisms that can reproduce to produce fertile offspring
An internationally agreed system where scientific names have two parts showing genus and species
A jelly-like material within the cell in which reactions occur, containing organelles
A thin membrane that surrounds the cell and controls entry and exit of substances
An organism that causes disease, including bacteria and viruses

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give similarities AND differences between two things
Show working clearly and include units in your answer

modelAnswer.question: State three differences between plant and animal cells. [3]
modelAnswer.answer: Plant cells have a cell wall made of cellulose, animal cells do not. Plant cells have chloroplasts, animal cells do not. Plant cells have a permanent vacuole, animal cells have small temporary vacuoles.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Cell wall present in plants only
Mark 2: Chloroplasts present in plants only
Mark 3: Permanent vacuole in plants vs small/temporary vacuoles in animals

commonMistakes: Confusing classification based on physical appearance rather than evolutionary relationships through DNA sequences
Thinking viruses are living organisms when they are non-cellular and cannot complete life processes
Mixing up vertebrate and arthropod characteristics - vertebrates have backbones, arthropods have exoskeletons
Forgetting that all seven life processes must be present for something to be classified as living
examinerTips: Remember the binomial system always has genus first, then species - like Homo sapiens for humans
DNA sequencing is more accurate than physical characteristics for classification as similar habitats can cause unrelated species to evolve similar traits
formulas: Magnification = size of image ÷ actual size of object
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Plant and animal cell diagrams showing nucleus, cytoplasm, cell membrane, mitochondria. Plant cell additionally shows cell wall, chloroplasts, and permanent vacuole. All parts must be clearly labelled.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q3 (4 marks) — they asked students to identify structures visible in an electron micrograph of onion root cells prepared using freeze-fracture technique and explain their appearance
O/N 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) — tested knowledge of which structures are present during specific stages of mitosis, specifically asking about centromeres in human muscle cells with 46 chromosomes
M/J 2023 P2 Q7 (3 marks) — required students to calculate cell diameter from photomicrographs and identify what additional information was needed for measurements in micrometers
O/N 2022 P1 Q12 (1 mark) — tested understanding of polysaccharide structure, specifically which properties are shared between starch and cellulose regarding monosaccharide units and glycosidic bonds
M/J 2024 P2 Q4 (5 marks) — showed photomicrograph of onion cells after osmotic treatment and asked students to identify what filled the spaces between cell wall and membrane

workedExample.question: An electron micrograph shows plant cells magnified ×15,000. The actual diameter of one cell nucleus is 8 μm. Calculate the diameter of the nucleus as it appears in the micrograph. Show your working. (3 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify the formula needed: Image size = Actual size × Magnification
Step 2: Convert actual size to appropriate units: 8 μm = 8 × 10⁻⁶ m
Step 3: Apply the magnification: Image size = 8 × 10⁻⁶ × 15,000
Step 4: Calculate: Image size = 0.12 m = 120 mm = 12 cm
workedExample.answer: Image size = Actual size × Magnification. Image size = 8 × 10⁻⁶ × 15,000 = 0.12 m = 12 cm
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct formula, 1 mark for correct substitution of values, 1 mark for correct final answer with appropriate units

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell structures' — always check whether the question refers to bacterial, plant, or animal cells before listing organelles
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to convert units in magnification calculations' — always ensure final answers are in the units requested in the question
Examiner Report 2024: 'Candidates often described function instead of structure' — when asked to identify cell structures, focus on what you can see, not what the organelle does
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw and label a typical animal cell showing: nucleus with nuclear envelope and nucleolus, cytoplasm, cell membrane, mitochondria, ribosomes, rough and smooth endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus, lysosomes, and centrosome. The diagram should show relative sizes accurately (nucleus ~10μm, mitochondria ~2μm) and include at least 8 labeled structures. Organelles should be drawn with correct internal structure - mitochondria with cristae, ER with attached ribosomes, nucleus with nuclear pores.
note_data.quickRecap: Living organisms are classified based on shared features and evolutionary relationships determined through DNA analysis. All living things undergo seven key processes and are grouped into five kingdoms based on cellular features. Classification uses the binomial naming system with genus and species names.
